<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] Three-file zone (1 source, 2 tests) is well-tested relative to its size; the focused scope makes this a low-risk, high-confidence module.
[observation] [info] data-loading-efficiency.test.ts signals deliberate performance validation for the data serving path, which is appropriate given this route is on the critical path for dashboard initialization.
[observation] [warning] web-unit imports web-application-core 7 times; since routes-data.ts is a server route handler, confirm these imports are type-only (stripped at compile time) and do not introduce a runtime dependency from server routing logic into viewer modules.
[relationship] [warning] routes-data.ts (server route handler, 3-file zone) imports 7 symbols from the 338-file web-application-core zone which contains viewer components; the asymmetric edge count (7 in from the small zone, 1 out from the large zone) indicates routes-data.ts is consuming viewer-side abstractions — these must be verified as type-only imports to prevent server code from acquiring a Preact runtime dependency.
[suggestion] [info] data-loading-efficiency.test.ts tests pagination API correctness (limit/offset parameters, Content-Length headers, metadata preservation) rather than execution speed or memory efficiency. Rename to data-pagination-contract.test.ts to accurately reflect the test's scope and make it discoverable to contributors auditing the pagination API rather than performance characteristics.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- 7 imports from this zone into web-application-core suggest routes-data.ts or its test files depend on viewer-side types or utilities; for a server route handler, these should be type-only imports (stripped at build time) to avoid bundling server code into the viewer.
- The data-loading-efficiency.test.ts file indicates deliberate performance validation for this route, which is appropriate since it serves large analysis artifacts (inventory, imports, zones JSON) on every dashboard load.
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) with zero coupling reflects a focused, side-effect-free route handler — reads pre-built files and streams them without mutating shared state.
- web-unit imports web-application-core 7 times; since routes-data.ts is a server route handler, confirm these imports are type-only (stripped at compile time) and do not introduce a runtime dependency from server routing logic into viewer modules.
- data-loading-efficiency.test.ts signals deliberate performance validation for the data serving path, which is appropriate given this route is on the critical path for dashboard initialization.
- Three-file zone (1 source, 2 tests) is well-tested relative to its size; the focused scope makes this a low-risk, high-confidence module.
- web-data-routes and web-application-core form the only bidirectional zone pair (7 in + 1 out) where the lower-mass zone (3 files) drives the majority of edges into the higher-mass zone (338 files); this inversion of the expected hub→spoke direction suggests routes-data.ts reaches into viewer-side types rather than confining itself to server-side contracts.
- routes-data.ts (server route handler, 3-file zone) imports 7 symbols from the 338-file web-application-core zone which contains viewer components; the asymmetric edge count (7 in from the small zone, 1 out from the large zone) indicates routes-data.ts is consuming viewer-side abstractions — these must be verified as type-only imports to prevent server code from acquiring a Preact runtime dependency.
- data-loading-efficiency.test.ts tests pagination correctness and API contract conformance at scale (200–500 items, Content-Length headers, metadata preservation) rather than latency or throughput — the file name contains 'efficiency' but the test content is a pagination API contract test. This naming mismatch means contributors looking for performance benchmarks will not find them, and those reading the file expecting correctness tests may be confused by the efficiency framing.
- data-loading-efficiency.test.ts tests pagination API correctness (limit/offset parameters, Content-Length headers, metadata preservation) rather than execution speed or memory efficiency. Rename to data-pagination-contract.test.ts to accurately reflect the test's scope and make it discoverable to contributors auditing the pagination API rather than performance characteristics.
- [call graph] 9 internal calls, 9 outgoing, 2 incoming (cohesion: 0.5, coupling: 0.5)

</insights>
